title: Cisco ACI Policy TCAM Footprint: eqptcapacityPolUsage5min Pressure, actrlRule Proliferation Correlation, and F0952 Program-Failure Triangulation
exclusions: Per-rule permit-vs-deny traffic ratios and actrlRuleHit5min packet/byte analytics (**UC-18.1.9**); contract hit-volume baselines without hardware CAM math (**UC-18.1.4** alone); fabric-wide rolled-up health dials without per-leaf resource counters (**UC-18.1.1**); L3Out border peer stability (**UC-18.1.14**); fabric membership retirement signals (**UC-18.1.12**); standalone NX-OS ACL sizing outside APIC context (**UC-18.4.x**).
evidence: Saved search `aci_uc1815_policy_tcam_pressure` on `index=cisco_aci` merging `cisco:aci:capacity`, `cisco:aci:contract_stats`, `cisco:aci:fault`, `cisco:aci:syslog`, `cisco:aci:health` with drill tokens `node_key`, `pol_pct_now`, `approx_rule_dns`, `tcam_fault_flag`, and `est_hours_to_90`.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab leaf, vzFilter additions drive polUsageCum toward polUsageCap while distinct actrlRule DNs climb; within the same 15m bucket Splunk raises risk>=80 and faultInst shows F0952 with matching descr text.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Traffic-plane ratios oscillate (UC-18.1.9 contract analytics) but eqptcapacityPolUsage5min stays below 40% with zero F0952 across 6h—this UC should stay quiet aside from informational tiles.

dataSources: `index=cisco_aci` (rename per governance) carries five sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cisco:aci:capacity` from REST `GET /api/class/eqptcapacityPolUsage5min.json` carrying polUsageCum, polUsageCap, polUsageBase; `sourcetype=cisco:aci:contract_stats` from `GET /api/class/actrlRule.json` exposing compiled actrlRule DNs; `sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault` for structured faultInst rows including F0952/F1585; `sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog` via **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (Splunkbase **4022**); `sourcetype=cisco:aci:health` for optional fabric score lanes; collectors typically use **rest_ta** (Splunkbase **1546**) on 120–300s cadence with aaaLogin/aaaRefresh sessions.
app: **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022), package `Splunk_TA_cisco-aci`) for `cisco:aci:syslog`; **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**rest_ta**, [Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for `cisco:aci:capacity`, `cisco:aci:contract_stats`, `cisco:aci:fault`, optional `cisco:aci:health`.
spl:
index=cisco_aci sourcetype IN ("cisco:aci:capacity","cisco:aci:contract_stats","cisco:aci:fault","cisco:aci:syslog","cisco:aci:health") earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval lane=case(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:capacity","cap",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:contract_stats","rule",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:fault","fi",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog","sy",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:health","hl",1=1,"misc")
| rex field=dn "(?i)/topology/pod-(?<pod_id>[^/]+)"
| eval node_key=lower(trim(coalesce(pod_id,nodeId,node_id,lc_slot,lcSlot,fabricPathDn,"")))
| rex field=node_key "(?i)node-(?<node_id>\d+)"
| eval node_key=if(isnotnull(node_id), "node-".node_id, node_key)
| eval pol_cum=tonumber(coalesce(polUsageCum,pol_usage_cum,usageCum,""))
| eval pol_cap=tonumber(coalesce(polUsageCap,pol_usage_cap,usageCap,capCum,""))
| eval pol_pct=if(pol_cap>0 AND isnotnull(pol_cum), round(100*pol_cum/pol_cap,3), null())
| eval pol_free=if(isnotnull(pol_cap) AND isnotnull(pol_cum), pol_cap-pol_cum, null())
| eval rule_dn=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,rule_dn_raw,"")))
| eval actrl_marker=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:contract_stats" AND match(rule_dn,"actrlrule"),1,0)
| eval tenant_tag=if(actrl_marker=1, mvindex(split(rule_dn,"tn-"),1), null())
| eval tenant_tag=if(isnotnull(tenant_tag), mvindex(split(tenant_tag,"/"),0), null())
| eval fault_code=upper(trim(coalesce(code,faultCode,fault_code,"")))
| eval tcam_fault=if((lane IN ("fi","sy")) AND (fault_code IN ("F0952","F1585") OR match(_raw,"(?i)\bF0952\b|tcam|policy[- ]?cam|programming failed")),1,0)
| eval health_score=if(lane=="hl", tonumber(coalesce(healthScore,overallHealthScore,score,"")), null())
| bin _time span=15m as win15
| stats values(pod_id) as pods latest(pol_pct) as pol_pct_now latest(pol_free) as pol_free_now latest(pol_cap) as pol_cap_now latest(pol_cum) as pol_cum_now max(eval(if(lane=="cap",pol_pct,null()))) as pol_pct_max15 min(eval(if(lane=="cap",pol_free,null()))) as pol_free_min15 sum(actrl_marker) as actrl_rule_events max(tcam_fault) as tcam_fault_flag earliest(eval(if(lane=="hl",health_score,null()))) as health_first latest(eval(if(lane=="hl",health_score,null()))) as health_last dc(eval(if(actrl_marker=1,rule_dn,null()))) as approx_rule_dns values(sourcetype) as src_mix count as evt_volume by node_key win15
| where isnotnull(node_key) AND node_key!=""
| streamstats current=f window=2 global=f latest(pol_pct_now) as prev_pct by node_key
| eval pct_delta15=round(pol_pct_now-prev_pct,3)
| eval burn_rate=if(isnotnull(pct_delta15) AND pct_delta15>0, pct_delta15, null())
| eval est_hours_to_90=if(isnotnull(burn_rate) AND burn_rate>0, round((90-pol_pct_now)/burn_rate,1), null())
| where pol_pct_now>=70 OR pol_pct_max15>=75 OR pol_free_min15<=256 OR tcam_fault_flag>0 OR (isnotnull(est_hours_to_90) AND est_hours_to_90<=48)
| eval risk=case(tcam_fault_flag>0,100, pol_pct_now>=90,90, pol_free_min15<=128,85, isnotnull(est_hours_to_90) AND est_hours_to_90<=24,80, pol_pct_now>=75,70, 1=1,55)
| sort 0 - risk - pol_pct_now - tcam_fault_flag
| head 200
| table win15 node_key pods pol_pct_now pol_pct_max15 pct_delta15 pol_cum_now pol_cap_now pol_free_min15 approx_rule_dns actrl_rule_events tcam_fault_flag health_first health_last est_hours_to_90 risk evt_volume src_mix
description: Watches Cisco ACI policy CAM (hardware TCAM) consumption on Nexus 9000 leaf ASICs using REST-sampled **eqptcapacityPolUsage5min** metrics (**polUsageCum**, **polUsageCap**, **polUsageBase**), joins per-leaf **compiled actrlRule** cardinality as a contract-and-filter proliferation signal, projects short-horizon burn toward fixed ceilings, and triangulates **F0952**/related faults plus TCAM-oriented syslog so programming failures surface before silent policy drops appear as application brownouts.
value: When policy tables fill, APIC can accept changes that never fully program—east-west flows begin failing in ways that look like missing contracts or noisy segmentation reviews, draining hours from the wrong war rooms. Pairing normalized **pol_pct** curves with **actrlRule** growth gives capacity owners a ledger pegged to vendor-published hardware fields, while **F0952** pulls those failures out of background noise. Hours-to-threshold forecasts turn last-minute fire drills into ordered filter consolidation, leaf SKU upgrades, or change freezes tied to real remaining headroom.
implementation: Deploy **rest_ta** (Splunkbase **1546**) HTTPS polls for **eqptcapacityPolUsage5min** and **actrlRule** into **cisco_aci** alongside **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (**4022**) syslog normalization. Stamp **collector_id**, optional **CMDB** ceiling lookup, schedule **`aci_uc1815_policy_tcam_pressure`** every **15m**, route **tcam_fault_flag** pages to fabric L3, and suppress planned work with **`aci_tcam_maint.csv`**.
visualization: (1) timechart — **pol_pct_now** per **node_key** with **75/90** reference bands and **tcam_fault_flag** overlays. (2) combo chart — **approx_rule_dns** bars with **pol_free_min15** line. (3) table — top **risk** rows including **est_hours_to_90**, **pods**, **src_mix**. (4) single-value — leaf count above **85%** policy CAM fill. (5) drilldown — opens neighboring search on **`cisco:aci:syslog`** for HAL / policy-stats strings tied to the same **node_key**.
schema: CIM **Performance** carries numeric **pol_pct_now**, **pol_free_min15**, and slope-style burn measures; **Alerts** frames **faultInst** rows plus enriched syslog markers as alert objects for routing workflows.

references.title: Cisco APIC REST API Configuration Guide (Release 6.0(x))
Cisco APIC Managed Object Reference — eqptcapacityPolUsage5min
Cisco APIC Troubleshooting Guide — Policies and Contracts (6.0(x))

knownFalsePositives:
• **polusage_dual_feed_overlap** — Two REST collectors hit the same **APIC** cluster with different **host** headers but land in one **index**, doubling **eqptcapacityPolUsage5min** rows so **pol_pct_now** reads high. Distinguish by **collector_id** in **host** or **inputs.conf** stanza names; deduplicate with `| dedup _time dn` or pause the stale input.

• **inventory_sku_stale_after_rma** — **CMDB** still lists **9364C** after an **RMA** replaced a leaf with **93180YC-FX**, so static ceiling math disagrees with **polUsageCap**. Distinguish with serial-level refresh; apply `lookup aci_leaf_tcam_ceiling.csv` only when **last_seen** is inside **24h**.

• **apic_leader_stats_pause** — During **APIC** **HA** leader moves, capacity samples pause while faults stay quiet, producing a flat **pol_pct** staircase. Distinguish with **cisco:aci:health** drops tied to **apicHaRole** churn; time-bound suppress **10m** after leadership settles using **inputlookup aci_apic_ha_events.csv** where **suppress=1**.

• **image_tcam_repartition_wave** — Some **ACI** releases rebalance **ingress** and **egress** **policy** regions after reload, so **polUsageCum** steps without new **vzBrCP** churn. Distinguish when every leaf in a **pod** spikes the same hour with one **change** ticket; honor **inputlookup aci_image_maint.csv** keyed by **node_key**.

• **span_redirect_borrow** — **SPAN**, **ERSPAN**, or **service-graph redirect** carve **TCAM** from the shared **policy** bank contract views ignore. Distinguish by auditing **monfv** sessions in **APIC**; tag rows **span_context=1** and add `where span_context=0` for paging tied to contract growth.

• **capacity_lane_missing** — **actrlRule** feeds exist without **`cisco:aci:capacity`**, so operators see rule counts but no **pol_pct**. Treat as a **data-quality** gap: page the **platform** team via a **scheduled** conformance search, not fabric engineering.

• **f1585_warmup_shadow** — **F1585** may flash during **line-card** startup while **F0952** stays zero and **policy-mgr** counters recover. Distinguish when **fault** **lifecycle** clears inside two polls and **CLI** **policy-stats** is healthy; filter `where fault_duration_seconds>300` after **raised/cleared** math.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• APIC on 6.0(x)–6.2(x) with HTTPS 443/tcp reachable from Splunk heavy forwarders; dedicate a **read-only REST account** whose **aaaUser RBAC** can query **eqptcapacityPolUsage5min**, **actrlRule**, **faultInst**, and **topSystem** without admin write paths.
• Install Splunk_TA_cisco-aci ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022), directory `Splunk_TA_cisco-aci`) wherever APIC syslog terminates so `sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog` preserves **F0952 policy programming** text.
• Deploy rest_ta ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) stanzas for `GET /api/class/eqptcapacityPolUsage5min.json?rsp-subtree=full` and `GET /api/class/actrlRule.json?rsp-subtree=no-unresolved` on **120–300 second** cadence; shorter polls sharpen **burn-rate math** but increase **HTTP 429** risk during backups.
• Maintain `aci_leaf_tcam_ceiling.csv` with SKU guidance (**93180YC-FX ~4K**, **9364C ~8K**, **9348GC-FXP ~2K** compiled-entry hints keyed by model)—sanity-check Splunk math, never override **polUsageCap** from the MO.
• Keep collector **TLS trust** aligned with APIC certificates; proxies that strip **SNI** break Python REST clients with subtle failures.

Step 1 — Configure data collection (five sourcetypes)
(1) `cisco:aci:capacity` — Poll `eqptcapacityPolUsage5min` into `index=cisco_aci`; extract **polUsageCum**, **polUsageCap**, **polUsageBase**, **dn**, **modTs**, and line-card labels. Expect **ingress and egress policy slices** per leaf.
(2) `cisco:aci:contract_stats` — Poll `/api/class/actrlRule.json` with **attribute-only** replies when possible; retain tn-{tenant}, brc-{contract}, subj-{subject} DN segments for **contract proliferation** joins.
(3) `cisco:aci:fault` — Ingest **faultInst** JSON highlighting **F0952** and companion **F1585**; keep **descr**, **dn**, **severity**, **cause**, **lc**.
(4) `cisco:aci:syslog` — APIC/leaf messages referencing **TCAM**, **policy CAM**, **actrl**, or **HAL phase4**; normalize timestamps to **UTC**.
(5) `cisco:aci:health` — Optional **fabric health** lane (fvOverallHealth family) to explain isolated leaf alarms while dashboards stay green.
**Sizing** — sub-10k events/day for capacity on 180s polling across ~100 leaf fabrics; wide actrlRule pulls dominate cost—tighten **rsp-subtree** and log **collector_id** on every row for dedup audits.

Step 2 — Saved search, alert, SPL
Save `aci_uc1815_policy_tcam_pressure` on cron `*/15 * * * *` with earliest=-6h@m. Page when **risk>=80** or **tcam_fault_flag>0**; throttle **15 minutes** per node_key.

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype IN ("cisco:aci:capacity","cisco:aci:contract_stats","cisco:aci:fault","cisco:aci:syslog","cisco:aci:health") earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval lane=case(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:capacity","cap",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:contract_stats","rule",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:fault","fi",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog","sy",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:health","hl",1=1,"misc")
| rex field=dn "(?i)/topology/pod-(?<pod_id>[^/]+)"
| eval node_key=lower(trim(coalesce(pod_id,nodeId,node_id,lc_slot,lcSlot,fabricPathDn,"")))
| rex field=node_key "(?i)node-(?<node_id>\d+)"
| eval node_key=if(isnotnull(node_id), "node-".node_id, node_key)
| eval pol_cum=tonumber(coalesce(polUsageCum,pol_usage_cum,usageCum,""))
| eval pol_cap=tonumber(coalesce(polUsageCap,pol_usage_cap,usageCap,capCum,""))
| eval pol_pct=if(pol_cap>0 AND isnotnull(pol_cum), round(100*pol_cum/pol_cap,3), null())
| eval pol_free=if(isnotnull(pol_cap) AND isnotnull(pol_cum), pol_cap-pol_cum, null())
| eval rule_dn=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,rule_dn_raw,"")))
| eval actrl_marker=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:contract_stats" AND match(rule_dn,"actrlrule"),1,0)
| eval tenant_tag=if(actrl_marker=1, mvindex(split(rule_dn,"tn-"),1), null())
| eval tenant_tag=if(isnotnull(tenant_tag), mvindex(split(tenant_tag,"/"),0), null())
| eval fault_code=upper(trim(coalesce(code,faultCode,fault_code,"")))
| eval tcam_fault=if((lane IN ("fi","sy")) AND (fault_code IN ("F0952","F1585") OR match(_raw,"(?i)\bF0952\b|tcam|policy[- ]?cam|programming failed")),1,0)
| eval health_score=if(lane=="hl", tonumber(coalesce(healthScore,overallHealthScore,score,"")), null())
| bin _time span=15m as win15
| stats values(pod_id) as pods latest(pol_pct) as pol_pct_now latest(pol_free) as pol_free_now latest(pol_cap) as pol_cap_now latest(pol_cum) as pol_cum_now max(eval(if(lane=="cap",pol_pct,null()))) as pol_pct_max15 min(eval(if(lane=="cap",pol_free,null()))) as pol_free_min15 sum(actrl_marker) as actrl_rule_events max(tcam_fault) as tcam_fault_flag earliest(eval(if(lane=="hl",health_score,null()))) as health_first latest(eval(if(lane=="hl",health_score,null()))) as health_last dc(eval(if(actrl_marker=1,rule_dn,null()))) as approx_rule_dns values(sourcetype) as src_mix count as evt_volume by node_key win15
| where isnotnull(node_key) AND node_key!=""
| streamstats current=f window=2 global=f latest(pol_pct_now) as prev_pct by node_key
| eval pct_delta15=round(pol_pct_now-prev_pct,3)
| eval burn_rate=if(isnotnull(pct_delta15) AND pct_delta15>0, pct_delta15, null())
| eval est_hours_to_90=if(isnotnull(burn_rate) AND burn_rate>0, round((90-pol_pct_now)/burn_rate,1), null())
| where pol_pct_now>=70 OR pol_pct_max15>=75 OR pol_free_min15<=256 OR tcam_fault_flag>0 OR (isnotnull(est_hours_to_90) AND est_hours_to_90<=48)
| eval risk=case(tcam_fault_flag>0,100, pol_pct_now>=90,90, pol_free_min15<=128,85, isnotnull(est_hours_to_90) AND est_hours_to_90<=24,80, pol_pct_now>=75,70, 1=1,55)
| sort 0 - risk - pol_pct_now - tcam_fault_flag
| head 200
| table win15 node_key pods pol_pct_now pol_pct_max15 pct_delta15 pol_cum_now pol_cap_now pol_free_min15 approx_rule_dns actrl_rule_events tcam_fault_flag health_first health_last est_hours_to_90 risk evt_volume src_mix
```

Understanding this SPL
**Lanes** — cap=counters from `eqptcapacityPolUsage5min`; rule=compiled `actrlRule` cardinality; fi/sy=fault/syslog proof; hl=optional health context.
**node_key** — Built from pod/node DN regex; replace rex if MO paths differ; join `topSystem` when identities drift.
**pol_pct_now** — polUsageCum ÷ polUsageCap tracks **policy CAM percent**; subtract polUsageBase if your runbook mirrors CLI **`policy-stats`** reservations.
**approx_rule_dns** — Distinct rule DN count hints **TCAM arity growth**; join tenant_tag to show which contracts ballooned.
**pct_delta15 / burn_rate** — streamstats slope yields **est_hours_to_90**; treat the forecast as **early warning**, not a promise.
**tcam_fault_flag** — Any true value is **dataplane programming failure**—page fabric engineering and collect CLI proof before rollbacks.
**risk** — Sorting score only; map 55–100 to INFO→CRITICAL in alert workflows.

**Pipeline recap** — `bin` aligns REST jitter to **15m buckets**; `stats` merges all five sourcetypes per **node_key**; `streamstats` derives **burn slope**; final `where` drops steady-state noise unless a **fault** or **fast approach** flag is set.

Step 3 — Validate in APIC GUI and Nexus CLI
(1) APIC: Fabric → Inventory → Pod → Leaf capacity views should match **pol_pct_now within ±2%** after one poll cycle.
(2) Leaf CLI: `vsh_lc -c "show platform internal hal l2 phase4 policy-stats"` — HAL percent should track REST; drift implies **stale APIC cache** or **wrong controller target**.
(3) `show zoning-rule summary` totals should align with **approx_rule_dns** magnitude; huge gaps mean **incomplete actrlRule polling**.
(4) `show system internal policy-mgr stats` — stalled successes with live **F0952** indicates **hardware exhaustion**.
(5) `timechart count by sourcetype` over 24h—five steady feeds; **missing cisco:aci:capacity >1h** is a platform incident.

Step 4 — Dashboards, RBAC, runbooks
(1) Executive row — worst **pol_free_min15** plus count of leaves **≥85%** fill; red tile whenever **tcam_fault_flag>0**.
(2) Operator row — **timechart pol_pct_now** by node_key with **75/90** reference lines; secondary axis for **approx_rule_dns**.
(3) Drilldown — set **tok_tenant** before hopping to aaaModLR or contract_scope investigations.
(4) RBAC — only **aci_network_owner** edits macros `aci_tcam_warn` / `aci_tcam_crit`; noc_viewer stays read-only.
(5) Runbook — link internal **contract deduplication** guides and ESC template **UC-18.1.15**.

Step 5 — Visualization, alert design, troubleshooting
**Visualization** — Dashboard Studio worksheet: brush-linked leaf table + policy percent timeline; ranked lists beat pie charts.
**Alert routing** — **tcam_fault_flag** → fabric L3 on-call; burn-only (`est_hours_to_90`) → capacity L2 with forecast in subject.
**Suppression** — `inputlookup aci_tcam_maint.csv` on node_key + UTC window; require **change_record** for auto closure.

**Troubleshooting**
• **No capacity events** — check **aaaRefresh** failures, `python.log` for 403/429, corporate proxy auth loops.
• **Stuck at 0%** — leaf may be administratively down while CMDB still lists it—validate **fabricSt** using inventory MOs (see UC-18.1.12).
• **Fault before percent climbs** — honor **F0952** immediately; CLI evidence beats waiting for **90%** thresholds.
• **Too-high utilization** — duplicate REST feeds or **multi-valued pol_cap** — `| stats values(pol_cap) by node_key` exposes splits.
• **Spike after change** — correlate **aaaModLR** velocity to the same engineer; drive **filter consolidation** before SKU upgrades.
grandmaExplanation: We map how much spare room each rack switch still keeps for its traffic-rule notebook—when pages run low, new rules may not stick—so your crew gets a plain warning before problems masquerade as vague application timeouts.
hardware: Cisco APIC clusters (M3/M4/L4) managing Nexus 9000 ACI-mode leaf switches with finite ingress/egress policy TCAM banks for compiled actrlRule/actrlEntry programming; exact polUsageCap depends on SKU and image.
